name: 3Blue1Brown
description: Grant Sanderson's math visualization channel. Extraordinary animations that build genuine intuition for linear algebra, calculus, and neural networks.
scoring_notes: Gold standard for math education. Custom animation engine (manim), no filler, every video is a masterclass in visual explanation. Rewatch value is extremely high.
sample_videos: [{"title": "But what is a neural network?", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=aircAruvnKk"}, {"title": "The essence of linear algebra", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fNk_zzaMoSs"}]

name: Veritasium
description: Derek Muller explores science, engineering, and counterintuitive phenomena. High production value with a mix of interviews, experiments, and explainers.
scoring_notes: Excellent science communication. Occasional clickbait titles but content delivers. Some videos lean more entertainment than depth. Strong interview segments.
sample_videos: [{"title": "The Biggest Misconception About Electricity", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=bHIhgxav9LY"}, {"title": "How The Helicopter Was Invented", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ad8kkOLroCk"}]

name: Kurzgesagt - In a Nutshell
description: Munich-based studio producing animated science explainers. Topics range from astrophysics to biology, with a dedicated research team and source documents.
scoring_notes: Stunning animation quality. Sources cited. Some topics oversimplified for mass audience. Research team adds credibility. Occasionally sensationalist framing.
sample_videos: [{"title": "The Egg - A Short Story", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=h6fcK_fRYaI"}, {"title": "The Immune System Explained", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zQGOcOUBi6s"}]

name: PBS Space Time
description: Hosted by Matt O'Dowd, tackles advanced astrophysics and quantum mechanics without dumbing down. One of the few channels that presents actual equations and math.
scoring_notes: Rare channel that respects viewer intelligence. Handles tensor math and general relativity properly. Production modest but content is university-lecture level. Essential for physics enthusiasts.
sample_videos: [{"title": "The True Nature of Matter and Mass", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gSKzgpt4HBU"}, {"title": "Is Gravity An Illusion?", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=NblR01hHK6U"}]

name: SmarterEveryDay
description: Destin Sandlin combines slow-motion footage with genuine curiosity. Aerospace engineer by training, covers rockets, ballistics, biology, and laminar flow.
scoring_notes: Authentic curiosity-driven approach. High-speed camera work is unique. Access to NASA/military facilities adds value. Occasional sponsored segments but clearly marked.
sample_videos: [{"title": "The Backwards Brain Bicycle", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MFzDaBzBlL0"}, {"title": "How Do Nuclear Submarines Make Oxygen?", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=g3Ud6mHdhlQ"}]

name: Steve Mould
description: British science communicator demonstrating physics through hands-on experiments. Known for the Mould Effect (self-siphoning beads) and approachable explanations.
scoring_notes: Great at making physics tangible. Less polished production than top-tier but compensates with genuine demonstrations. Good signal-to-noise ratio.
sample_videos: [{"title": "The Self-Siphoning Beads", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_dQJBBklpQQ"}, {"title": "Sand Fleas are Incredible", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qZb7Fg8JRCU"}]

name: Sabine Hossenfelder
description: Theoretical physicist offering critical takes on modern physics, science funding, and popular science misconceptions. Unapologetically contrarian.
scoring_notes: Rare critical voice in physics YouTube. Willing to call out hype (string theory, quantum computing). Production is basic but content depth is excellent. Some topics outside her expertise are weaker.
sample_videos: [{"title": "I stopped satisfsatisfying the algorithm", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jMGv3GQhOcY"}, {"title": "The Trouble With Many Worlds", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LgIw-JRMhIQ"}]

name: Domain of Science
description: Dominic Walliman creates detailed maps of scientific fields. Each 'Map of' video visualizes an entire discipline and its connections.
scoring_notes: Unique niche: science cartography. Great for orienting learners in a new field. Limited depth per topic by design. Excellent reference material.
sample_videos: [{"title": "The Map of Mathematics", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OmJ-4B-mS-Y"}, {"title": "The Map of Physics", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ZihywtixUYo"}]

name: Up and Atom
description: Jade Tan-Holmes explains quantum physics, math puzzles, and computer science. Clear teaching style aimed at making advanced concepts accessible.
scoring_notes: Solid educational content. Good at breaking down complex topics. Less production polish than top channels. Covers well-trodden topics but explains them clearly.
sample_videos: [{"title": "Quantum Entanglement Explained", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JFozGfxmi8A"}, {"title": "The Halting Problem Explained", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=92WHN-pAFCs"}]

name: MinutePhysics
description: Henry Reich's hand-drawn physics explanations in short format. Pioneer of the science explainer genre on YouTube.
scoring_notes: Efficient format: high signal-to-noise in short videos. Depth limited by format. Hand-drawn style is iconic but dated. Some older videos haven't aged well scientifically.
sample_videos: [{"title": "What is Gravity?", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=p_o4aY7xkXg"}, {"title": "Is It Better to Walk or Run in the Rain?", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3MqYE2UuN24"}]

name: Arvin Ash
description: Physics and cosmology explained with simple animations. Covers quantum mechanics, relativity, and the nature of reality for a general audience.
scoring_notes: Accessible explanations of advanced physics. Production quality is modest. Topics overlap heavily with larger channels. Good entry point for beginners.
sample_videos: [{"title": "What is the Double Slit Experiment?", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=A9tKncAdlHQ"}, {"title": "What happened before the Big Bang?", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dr6nNvw55C4"}]

name: Numberphile
description: Brady Haran interviews mathematicians about fascinating number theory, puzzles, and proofs. Brown paper explanations from world-class academics.
scoring_notes: Access to top mathematicians is the key asset. Brown paper format is iconic. Some videos are more accessible than others. Massive archive of mathematical topics.
sample_videos: [{"title": "The Riemann Hypothesis", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=d6c6uIyieoo"}, {"title": "TREE(3) - The Largest Number", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3P6DWAwwViU"}]

name: Thought Emporium
description: DIY biohacking, neuroscience, and materials science. Growing graphene at home, brain-computer interfaces, and genetic engineering in a garage lab.
scoring_notes: Ambitious DIY science that pushes boundaries. Actually grows graphene and builds BCIs. Production is raw but authenticity compensates. Safety concerns sometimes glossed over.
sample_videos: [{"title": "Growing Graphene at Home", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=u68FBjH-0cI"}, {"title": "Building a Brain Computer Interface", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GakjjdDVul8"}]

name: Fireship
description: Jeff Delaney's rapid-fire web dev channel. Famous for '100 seconds' format and weekly tech news with dry humor. Extremely high information density.
scoring_notes: Pioneered the ultra-dense tech explainer format. Great signal-to-noise but depth is sacrificed for speed. Excellent for staying current. Some takes are surface-level.
sample_videos: [{"title": "God-Tier Developer Roadmap", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pEfrdAtAmqk"}, {"title": "100+ Computer Science Concepts Explained", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-uleG_Vecis"}]

name: ThePrimeagen
description: Former Netflix engineer. Vim evangelist, Rust advocate, and dev culture commentator. Mix of live coding, hot takes, and reaction content.
scoring_notes: Authentic developer voice. High entertainment value but signal-to-noise varies with reaction content. Deep systems knowledge when he dives in. Strong community influence.
sample_videos: [{"title": "Vim As Your Editor", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=X6AR2RMB5tE"}, {"title": "Software Engineering is Dying", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9al167AkHBg"}]

name: Computerphile
description: University of Nottingham's computer science channel. Academics explain algorithms, security, AI, and computing history on a whiteboard.
scoring_notes: Academic rigor is the strength. Production is deliberately minimal (whiteboard). Excellent archive of CS fundamentals. Multiple expert presenters add variety.
sample_videos: [{"title": "Dijkstra's Algorithm", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GazC3A4OQTE"}, {"title": "How Bletchley Park Broke Enigma", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=RzWB5jL5RX0"}]

name: Ben Eater
description: Builds computers from scratch on breadboards. 8-bit CPU series is legendary. Teaches digital logic, networking, and graphics at the transistor level.
scoring_notes: Unmatched depth in hardware education. Building a CPU from NAND gates is a unique contribution. Zero filler. Patient, thorough teaching style. Videos remain relevant indefinitely.
sample_videos: [{"title": "Building an 8-bit breadboard computer!", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=HyznrdDSSGM"}, {"title": "How do CRCs work?", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=izG7qT0EpBw"}]

name: George Hotz
description: Hacker who jailbroke the iPhone and PS3. Now builds self-driving cars (comma.ai) and tinygrad. Raw, unedited coding streams with deep systems insight.
scoring_notes: Brilliant engineer, raw presentation. Production is stream-quality. Signal-to-noise is low (long streams) but peaks are extremely insightful. Not for casual viewers.
sample_videos: [{"title": "Programming the tinygrad framework", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Gn_IMXp5x2E"}, {"title": "I built a self-driving car", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Hxoke1lDJ9w"}]

name: NetworkChuck
description: Networking and cybersecurity tutorials with high energy. Covers Docker, Linux, Python, and home lab setups for beginners to intermediate users.
scoring_notes: Engaging presenter, good production. Content is beginner-friendly but rarely goes deep. High energy style can feel forced. Good gateway to networking topics.
sample_videos: [{"title": "you need to learn Docker RIGHT NOW!!", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=eGz9DS-aIeY"}, {"title": "FREE CCNA 200-301 Course", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=S7MNX_UD7vY"}]

name: Jeff Geerling
description: Raspberry Pi, Ansible, and homelab expert. Methodical hardware reviews, Kubernetes at home, and Petabyte-scale projects. Data-driven approach.
scoring_notes: Extremely thorough testing methodology. No fluff. Ansible and Kubernetes content is reference-quality. Hardware reviews are genuinely useful. Community contributions valued.
sample_videos: [{"title": "I built a mass Raspberry Pi cluster", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kgVz4-SEhbE"}, {"title": "Petabyte Project - How I store my data", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DgX34AQg1JQ"}]

name: Low Level Learning
description: Systems programming focused: C, Rust, assembly, and OS internals. Short, punchy videos that demystify low-level computing concepts.
scoring_notes: Good niche in systems programming education. Videos are concise and well-structured. Some clickbait titles but content delivers. Fills an important gap.
sample_videos: [{"title": "you will mass this satisfying question about C", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=w3_e9vZj7D8"}, {"title": "how mass the Linux kernel works", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=KwixKYPbFJA"}]

name: LiveOverflow
description: Security researcher explaining hacking, CTFs, and vulnerability research. Deep technical walkthroughs of real exploits and bug bounty findings.
scoring_notes: Genuine expertise in security research. Shows real exploitation process, not just results. Some videos require prior knowledge. Excellent for aspiring security researchers.
sample_videos: [{"title": "How a mass works in software", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1S0aBV-Waeo"}, {"title": "Hacking Google - The Movie", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=aOGFY1R4QQ4"}]

name: ArjanCodes
description: Python design patterns, software architecture, and clean code practices. Former academic turned YouTuber focusing on practical software engineering.
scoring_notes: Solid Python education. Design pattern content is useful but not groundbreaking. Good production for coding tutorials. Some content feels repetitive across videos.
sample_videos: [{"title": "8 Design Patterns EVERY Developer Should Know", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=tAuRQs_d9F8"}, {"title": "Uncle Bob's SOLID Principles Made Easy", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pTB30aXS77U"}]

name: Sebastian Lague
description: Coding adventures in procedural generation, pathfinding, and simulations. Builds complex systems from scratch with beautiful visualizations.
scoring_notes: Coding Adventures series is exceptional. Beautiful visualizations of complex algorithms. Patient, thorough approach. Each video is a journey through implementation. Highly rewatchable.
sample_videos: [{"title": "Coding Adventure: Simulating Fluids", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rSKMYc1CQHE"}, {"title": "Coding Adventure: Ray Marching", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Cp5WWtMoeKg"}]

name: Two Minute Papers
description: Karoly Zsolnai-Feher summarizes AI and computer graphics research papers. 'What a time to be alive!' Tracks cutting-edge ML, rendering, and simulation research.
scoring_notes: Great for staying current with AI research. Summaries are accessible. Enthusiasm is infectious. Some papers are oversimplified. High output keeps up with rapid field.
sample_videos: [{"title": "OpenAI GPT-4 Is Here!", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=--khbXchTeE"}, {"title": "NVIDIA's New AI Generates Faces", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kSLJriaOumA"}]

name: Practical Engineering
description: Grady Hillhouse explains civil and structural engineering with physical models. Bridges, dams, water systems, and infrastructure failures made tangible.
scoring_notes: Physical models are a killer differentiator. Zero filler, every second counts. Civil engineering is underserved on YouTube and he owns the niche. Consistently excellent.
sample_videos: [{"title": "Why City Water Still Isn't Safe to Drink", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ErbJlYjNHnE"}, {"title": "How Bridges Carry Weight", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oVOnRPefcno"}]

name: Real Engineering
description: Brian McManus covers aerospace, civil, and mechanical engineering. High-quality animations explaining how things work, from jet engines to rockets.
scoring_notes: Polished production with good research. Aerospace content is particularly strong. Occasional sponsored content but well-integrated. Good balance of depth and accessibility.
sample_videos: [{"title": "How NASA Reinvented the Wheel", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vSNtifE0Z2Q"}, {"title": "The Insane Engineering of the SR-71 Blackbird", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3hYSnyVLmGE"}]

name: Stuff Made Here
description: Shane Wighton builds absurd engineering projects: auto-aiming basketball hoops, hair-cutting robots, and explosive baseball bats. Software meets hardware.
scoring_notes: Incredibly original project concepts. Engineering process is well-documented. Some videos prioritize entertainment over education. Software/hardware integration is genuinely impressive.
sample_videos: [{"title": "Moving hsatisfying in basketball", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=myO8fxhDRgU"}, {"title": "I made mass hair cutting robot", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WlVS1skmDEA"}]

name: Mark Rober
description: Former NASA/Apple engineer building elaborate engineering projects. Glitter bombs for porch pirates, squirrel obstacle courses, and world records.
scoring_notes: Hollywood-level production on YouTube. Engineering is real but presentation prioritizes entertainment. Strong STEM advocacy. Some videos are more spectacle than education.
sample_videos: [{"title": "Glitterbomb Trap Catches Phone Scammers", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=h4T_LlK1VE4"}, {"title": "Building the Perfect Squirrel Proof Bird Feeder", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hFZFjoX2cGg"}]

name: Technology Connections
description: Alec Watson's deep dives into everyday technology. Heat pumps, traffic lights, dishwashers, and the surprisingly complex engineering behind mundane objects.
scoring_notes: Truly original niche: making everyday tech fascinating. Obsessive research into seemingly simple topics. Dry humor works. Heat pump videos genuinely changed public understanding.
sample_videos: [{"title": "Heat Pumps: the Future of Home Heating", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7J52mDjZzto"}, {"title": "The Dishwasher - A Surprising History", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_rBO8neWw04"}]

name: Engineering Explained
description: Jason Fenske explains automotive engineering with whiteboard math. Engine design, EV technology, aerodynamics, and drivetrain physics with actual calculations.
scoring_notes: Rare engineering channel that shows the math. Automotive focus with genuine engineering rigor. Whiteboard format is effective. Some videos overlap in content.
sample_videos: [{"title": "How Engines Work - 4 Stroke Internal Combustion", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DKF5dKo_r_Y"}, {"title": "The Truth About Hydrogen Cars", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=f7MzFfuNOtY"}]

name: Branch Education
description: Stunning 3D explainers of how devices work internally. Smartphone internals, CPU architecture, and mechanical watches rendered in incredible detail.
scoring_notes: 3D animation quality is extraordinary. Low upload frequency but each video is a reference piece. Topics are well-researched. Could go deeper on some subjects.
sample_videos: [{"title": "How does a Smartphone Work?", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nJQKEdZ3pNg"}, {"title": "How does Computer Hardware Work?", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=d86ws7mQYIg"}]

name: Undecided with Matt Ferrell
description: Matt Ferrell explores emerging energy and sustainability technology. Solar, batteries, hydrogen, and smart home tech with a balanced perspective.
scoring_notes: Good overview channel for clean energy tech. Sometimes overly optimistic about unproven technologies. Production is polished. Research could be more rigorous on technical claims.
sample_videos: [{"title": "This New Battery Could Change Everything", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=NkVRKtas1DI"}, {"title": "Why I went mass solar", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=YMFh0_CxnJI"}]

name: Applied Science
description: Ben Krasnow's home lab tackles electron microscopy, vacuum chambers, and semiconductor fabrication. Real engineering at an extraordinary hobbyist level.
scoring_notes: Genuine PhD-level work in a garage. Scanning electron microscope builds, chocolate tempering science. Utterly unique content. Production is raw but expertise is unquestionable.
sample_videos: [{"title": "Making a Scanning Electron Microscope", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=VdjYVF4a6iU"}, {"title": "The Science of Chocolate Tempering", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1wP0Uc8xnpU"}]

name: How Money Works
description: Animated economics and finance explainers. Covers wealth inequality, business models, and financial systems with well-researched scripts.
scoring_notes: Strong research with cited sources. Animation style is consistent and professional. Some clickbait framing but content is substantive. Good breadth of financial topics.
sample_videos: [{"title": "How The Ultra Rich Hide Their Money", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0MeRN7LE1LQ"}, {"title": "Why Stores Keep Getting Worse", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=grNGCbcGZVU"}]

name: Economics Explained
description: Macroeconomics analysis of countries, trade systems, and economic policies. Australian perspective on global economics with clear visual presentations.
scoring_notes: Good macro overviews. Country economy analyses are the strongest content. Some oversimplification of complex economic theory. Consistent quality and output.
sample_videos: [{"title": "The Economy of Germany", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=HPcttv9gD9g"}, {"title": "How China Became So Powerful", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qw-FLc7Z01Q"}]

name: Patrick Boyle
description: Former hedge fund manager offering finance commentary with sharp wit. Covers market events, fraud cases, and financial history with professional insight.
scoring_notes: Genuine industry expertise elevates content. Dry humor is distinctive. Production is simple but effective. Fraud case analyses are particularly excellent. Rare professional perspective on YouTube.
sample_videos: [{"title": "The Collapse of Credit Suisse", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=E2ANoeMBbo0"}, {"title": "Exposed: The Dark Side of Private Equity", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CnGfB4DYjlU"}]

name: Ben Felix
description: Evidence-based investing education. Portfolio manager who cites academic papers in every video. Destroys common investing myths with data.
scoring_notes: Gold standard for evidence-based finance content. Every claim backed by academic research. Zero marketing fluff. Changed how many people invest. Production could be more engaging.
sample_videos: [{"title": "The Problem with Trying to Beat the Market", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=AecvTErBQY8"}, {"title": "Investing in Dividend Stocks is a BAD Idea", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=f5j9v9dfinQ"}]

name: The Plain Bagel
description: Richard Coffin explains investing, financial products, and market events. CFA charterholder providing balanced, well-researched financial education.
scoring_notes: CFA credential adds credibility. Well-balanced perspectives on financial topics. Not flashy but consistently reliable. Good for intermediate investors.
sample_videos: [{"title": "What Caused the 2008 Financial Crisis?", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=N9YLta5Tr2A"}, {"title": "Index Funds vs ETFs: What's the Difference?", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Fxz17E3CoS4"}]

name: Money & Macro
description: Joeri Schasfoort explains central banking, monetary policy, and macro finance. PhD-level insights on how the financial system actually works.
scoring_notes: Academic rigor applied to monetary policy. Central banking content is among the best on YouTube. Some topics are niche but that's the appeal. Good balance of depth and accessibility.
sample_videos: [{"title": "How the Fed Creates Money", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=PBMnFOWr2wE"}, {"title": "Why Japan's Economy is Dying", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pSIFfKFfFHQ"}]

name: Aswath Damodaran
description: NYU Stern professor known as the 'Dean of Valuation'. Full university lectures and real-time company valuations freely available. Academic finance at its best.
scoring_notes: Literally the world authority on corporate valuation, teaching for free. Unmatched depth. Production is lecture-style (not YouTube-native) but content is irreplaceable. Every finance professional should watch.
sample_videos: [{"title": "Session 1: Introduction to Valuation", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=znmQ7oMiQrM"}, {"title": "Tesla Valuation: A 2023 Update", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=F6ICwMHOXzU"}]

name: Two Cents
description: PBS-produced personal finance channel. Covers credit scores, insurance, retirement planning, and common money decisions with clear animations.
scoring_notes: PBS backing ensures quality. Good for beginners. Topics are common personal finance questions. Well-produced but not deeply original. Solid gateway content.
sample_videos: [{"title": "Why You Should NOT Pay Off Your Mortgage Early", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=AKc01jo1qLw"}, {"title": "Should You Buy or Rent?", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q9Golcxjpi8"}]

name: Graham Stephan
description: Real estate investor and personal finance creator. Covers investing, real estate, and frugal living from a practitioner's perspective.
scoring_notes: Practical real estate experience is genuine. Content has become more clickbaity over time. Good for beginner personal finance. High volume dilutes quality. Entertainment over education.
sample_videos: [{"title": "How I Built 7 Streams of Income By Age 26", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QFrXTp3W29M"}, {"title": "Exposing The Truth About Renting vs Buying", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q9Golcxjpi8"}]

name: Kings and Generals
description: Animated military history with detailed battle maps. Covers ancient to modern warfare with strategic analysis and animated troop movements.
scoring_notes: Excellent battle animations and strategic analysis. Well-researched with good source material. High upload frequency maintains quality. Some series are stronger than others.
sample_videos: [{"title": "Battle of Cannae (216 BC)", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MroGPObEZzk"}, {"title": "Mongol Invasion of Europe", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=otHCpOiBkME"}]

name: Historia Civilis
description: Roman history told through colored squares and compelling narration. Makes the fall of the Republic feel like a thriller. Minimalist but mesmerizing.
scoring_notes: Proves you don't need fancy graphics. Colored squares representing armies/senators is iconic and weirdly effective. Script quality is outstanding. Deep primary source research. Infrequent uploads.
sample_videos: [{"title": "The Fall of the Roman Republic", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=AvplMmR_EKE"}, {"title": "Caesar Crosses the Rubicon", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yKf4MtZ4RQA"}]

name: OverSimplified
description: Animated history videos that make complex events funny and digestible. French Revolution, WW1/WW2, and Cold War covered with signature humor.
scoring_notes: Perfect gateway drug to history. Humor makes complex events memorable. Simplification is intentional (it's in the name) but well-executed. Very low upload frequency but each video is an event.
sample_videos: [{"title": "The French Revolution - OverSimplified (Part 1)", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8qRZcXIODNU"}, {"title": "WW2 - OverSimplified (Part 1)", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_uk_6vfqwTA"}]

name: Fall of Civilizations
description: Paul Cooper's documentary-length explorations of collapsed civilizations. Bronze Age, Roman Britain, Khmer Empire — each video is a 2-3 hour cinematic experience.
scoring_notes: Best long-form history content on YouTube, period. Each episode is documentary-quality. Narration is hypnotic. Research is exhaustive. These videos will be watched for decades.
sample_videos: [{"title": "The Bronze Age Collapse", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=B965f8AcNbw"}, {"title": "The Aztecs - A Clash of Worlds", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yH2JMhKBi_0"}]

name: Extra History
description: Animated history series from the Extra Credits team. Multi-part narratives covering Suleiman, the South Sea Bubble, and Justinian's Plague.
scoring_notes: Accessible and entertaining. Animation style is distinctive. 'Lies' episodes are a nice touch for self-correction. Research depth is adequate but not scholarly.
sample_videos: [{"title": "The South Sea Bubble - The Folly of Speculation", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=k1kndKWJKB8"}, {"title": "Justinian and Theodora - The Rise of an Empire", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=aNP9QdQKRig"}]

name: History Matters
description: Tenagain's short animated history videos answering specific historical questions. 3-5 minute format covering quirky and mainstream historical topics.
scoring_notes: Great for quick history bites. High output frequency. Limited depth by design. Animation style is charming. Good starting point that often sends viewers to deeper channels.
sample_videos: [{"title": "Why didn't the USSR annex Mongolia?", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kTfHpdH-qRc"}, {"title": "Why was Switzerland Neutral in WW2?", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=FILxoQyKzDg"}]

name: Invicta
description: Ancient military and cultural history. Roman legions, ancient warfare tactics, and daily life in historical civilizations with custom animations.
scoring_notes: Strong Roman content. Good balance of military and cultural history. Custom animations add value. Some overlap with Kings and Generals but more cultural focus.
sample_videos: [{"title": "Roman Legionary - Equipment and Training", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UfmiGMD75zM"}, {"title": "A Day in the Life of a Roman Soldier", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BwFof15Njas"}]

name: Dan Carlin - Hardcore History
description: Legendary podcast now on YouTube. Multi-hour deep dives into history's most dramatic moments. Blueprint for Armageddon (WW1) and Wrath of the Khans are masterpieces.
scoring_notes: Possibly the greatest history storyteller alive. Each episode is an audiobook-length event. Research is exhaustive. Production is audio-focused (podcast roots). Changed how millions think about history.
sample_videos: [{"title": "Blueprint for Armageddon I", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QDOU6vBfKJg"}, {"title": "Wrath of the Khans I", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hwOCqaIjHrI"}]

name: Voices of the Past
description: Primary source readings from history. Actual letters, diaries, and accounts from historical figures narrated with illustrations and context.
scoring_notes: Unique concept: letting historical people speak for themselves. Primary sources are carefully chosen. Illustrations add context. Great complement to analytical history channels.
sample_videos: [{"title": "Roman Soldier Describes Daily Life", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7pBYdU1dml4"}, {"title": "Japanese Samurai Describes First Contact with Europeans", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fmPsh1coxvY"}]

name: CaspianReport
description: Shirvan Neftchi's geopolitics channel from Azerbaijan. Covers global power dynamics, resource wars, and strategic chokepoints with maps and calm analysis.
scoring_notes: Non-Western perspective is refreshing and valuable. Deep strategic analysis. Topics are often ahead of mainstream news. Consistent quality over many years. Map-heavy format works well.
sample_videos: [{"title": "Why Turkey Is Invading Syria", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LTsGy0X7WIM"}, {"title": "The Geography of the Water Crisis", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WBM1vSKmooo"}]

name: TLDR News Global
description: Fast-turnaround news analysis. Covers elections, geopolitics, and policy with a balanced analytical lens. Multiple daily uploads on current events.
scoring_notes: Strong at rapid analysis of breaking news. Balances speed with accuracy. Some videos age poorly as events unfold. Good research team. Multiple sub-channels for regions.
sample_videos: [{"title": "Why the EU Is Falling Apart", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=D8WpgP5FCh0"}, {"title": "The Geopolitics of the South China Sea", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=djR5eAG2Q_M"}]

name: PolyMatter
description: Evan Edinger analyzes countries, economies, and companies through a geopolitical lens. Clean animations and well-structured arguments.
scoring_notes: Clean, well-produced analysis. Country deep-dives are the strongest content. Arguments are well-structured. Occasional oversimplification but generally reliable.
sample_videos: [{"title": "The Grand Theory of Amazon", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=NqKnG5OS0pY"}, {"title": "Why Singapore Is So Rich", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GQbfoHpSVjY"}]

name: RealLifeLore
description: Geography-based geopolitical analysis. Covers borders, territorial disputes, and how geography shapes politics with engaging map visualizations.
scoring_notes: Strong geography-geopolitics intersection. Map work is excellent. Some topics are surface-level. Good at making geographic constraints intuitive. Consistent output quality.
sample_videos: [{"title": "Why Russia Is Invading Ukraine", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=If61baWF4GE"}, {"title": "The Country That Has No Water", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MNoeSBo_ebs"}]

name: Wendover Productions
description: Sam Denby explains logistics, aviation, economics, and infrastructure. How supply chains, airlines, and global trade actually work behind the scenes.
scoring_notes: Logistics content is uniquely insightful. Aviation videos are excellent. Well-researched with good visualizations. Half Interesting (companion channel) is fun. Nebula partnership adds depth.
sample_videos: [{"title": "The Logistics of the US Election", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qmHFMGIoZJo"}, {"title": "The Business of Ski Resorts", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vpcUVOjUrKk"}]

name: Atlas Pro
description: Geography, geology, and biogeography explained with maps. Why deserts exist where they do, how mountains shape civilizations, and the logic of natural borders.
scoring_notes: Physical geography content is excellent. Unique niche that connects geology to human history. Good research depth. Some videos are more encyclopedic than analytical.
sample_videos: [{"title": "Why 94% of China Lives East of This Line", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=FAPTjceJ-DE"}, {"title": "Why Most of Africa Is Empty", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7RnoXEBusbw"}]

name: VisualPolitik EN
description: Spanish-origin channel covering global politics and economics. High-energy presentation of geopolitical topics with a libertarian-leaning perspective.
scoring_notes: High upload frequency dilutes quality. Some ideological bias in economic analysis. Production is decent. Good for overview but not deep analysis. Better for entertainment than research.
sample_videos: [{"title": "Why Is Switzerland So Rich?", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=G_RGC0IxIbk"}, {"title": "The Problem with the European Union", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yuQ6F6LpibA"}]

name: Good Times Bad Times
description: German-based geopolitics and defense channel. Covers NATO, European defense, and military strategy with current events analysis and maps.
scoring_notes: European defense perspective is underrepresented. Coverage of NATO and EU politics is useful. Production is basic. Some videos are too current-event-driven to have lasting value.
sample_videos: [{"title": "Why Germany Can't Build an Army", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=bQk9GJXk5lA"}, {"title": "NATO's Plan to Defend Europe", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5CjfT_DXg1Q"}]

name: Johnny Harris
description: Former Vox journalist covering borders, geopolitics, and investigative stories. Cinematic production with on-location reporting and map animations.
scoring_notes: Outstanding production quality. Some videos are well-researched, others are surface-level. Sponsored content sometimes blurs lines. Map animations are industry-leading. Style over substance at times.
sample_videos: [{"title": "How the US Stole the Middle East", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=iiRcY0Bkz6U"}, {"title": "Why All World Maps Are Wrong", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kIID5FDi2JQ"}]

name: Ali Abdaal
description: Doctor-turned-YouTuber covering productivity systems, book summaries, and content creation. High production value, increasingly business-focused.
scoring_notes: Polished production but increasingly formulaic. Earlier medical student content was stronger. Now more lifestyle/business than productivity. Good gateway but not deep.
sample_videos: [{"title": "My Honest Advice to Someone Who Wants to Start a YouTube Channel", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jVtkir80-Cs"}, {"title": "How I Type Really Fast (156 Words per Minute)", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1ArVtCQqQRE"}]

name: Thomas Frank
description: Study tips, Notion tutorials, and productivity systems. Practical advice for students and knowledge workers with step-by-step walkthroughs.
scoring_notes: Solid practical content for students. Notion content is well-executed. Not deeply original but reliable and actionable. Production quality is consistently high.
sample_videos: [{"title": "How to Build a Second Brain in Notion", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LXz8v6VtRss"}, {"title": "How to Study for Exams - Evidence-Based Tips", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ukLnPbIffxE"}]

name: Cal Newport
description: Georgetown CS professor and author of Deep Work. Covers focused work, digital minimalism, and knowledge worker productivity with academic rigor.
scoring_notes: Ideas are genuinely original and research-backed (Deep Work, Digital Minimalism). YouTube production is basic (podcast-style). Content quality makes up for low production value. Influential thinker.
sample_videos: [{"title": "Why You Should Quit Social Media", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3E7hkPZ-HTk"}, {"title": "The Deep Life Stack", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fkxjhJxL3Vs"}]

name: Matt D'Avella
description: Filmmaker covering minimalism, habits, and intentional living. Known for 30-day challenges and cinematic production quality. Netflix documentary Minimalism.
scoring_notes: Beautiful cinematography sets him apart visually. Content is lifestyle/self-improvement rather than deep productivity. 30-day challenges are engaging. More inspirational than informational.
sample_videos: [{"title": "I tried mass Dopamine Detox", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_vcGF3HqZXg"}, {"title": "A Day in My Life as mass Minimalist", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8P9mDPMCNKo"}]

name: Tiago Forte
description: Creator of the Building a Second Brain methodology. Personal knowledge management, note-taking systems, and creative output optimization.
scoring_notes: BASB methodology is genuinely original and useful. YouTube content sometimes feels like marketing for courses. Some videos are more promotional than educational. Core ideas are solid.
sample_videos: [{"title": "Building a Second Brain - Full Workshop", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=YOLELC1sENM"}, {"title": "The PARA Method Explained", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=T6AxkVBmyNs"}]

name: CGP Grey
description: Stick-figure explainer of systems, politics, and ideas. Voting systems, productivity philosophy (Spaceship You), and geographic oddities with obsessive research.
scoring_notes: Every video is a polished gem. Months/years between uploads but quality is extraordinary. 'Rules for Rulers' changed how people think about power. Systems thinking at its best.
sample_videos: [{"title": "Rules for Rulers", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rStL7niR7gs"}, {"title": "Spaceship You", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=snAhsXyO3Ck"}]

name: Atomic Shrimp
description: British creator covering scam-baiting, foraging, inventions, and lateral thinking. Eclectic mix of problem-solving content with dry British humor.
scoring_notes: Scam-baiting content is both entertaining and educational. Foraging and cooking segments are genuine. Lateral thinking approach to content. Unique voice in the space.
sample_videos: [{"title": "Scam Email Bait - The Adventure Continues", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=KMSM4sDL1HU"}, {"title": "Making a Meal from Foraged Ingredients", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XCLmMmS0AKA"}]

name: Pursuit of Wonder
description: Philosophical thought experiments and existential essays. Short films exploring meaning, consciousness, and the human condition with quiet narration.
scoring_notes: Atmospheric and thought-provoking. Short film format is distinctive. Some videos are more meditative than analytical. Good philosophical foundations without being academic.
sample_videos: [{"title": "The Egg - A Story About Everything", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ehRkOkJhz2c"}, {"title": "What If You Stopped Sleeping?", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nFTRwD85AQ4"}]

name: Exurb1a
description: Existential philosophy meets dark humor and sci-fi storytelling. Videos like 'Misery Was' and 'The Lantern' are genre-defining in YouTube essays.
scoring_notes: Truly unique voice on YouTube. Blends philosophy, humor, and fiction in a way nobody else does. Writing quality is exceptional. Some videos are life-changing for viewers. Controversial creator personally.
sample_videos: [{"title": "Misery Was", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=n__42UNIhvU"}, {"title": "27", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dLRLYPiaAoA"}]

name: Einzelganger
description: Stoicism, existentialism, and practical philosophy. Calm, focused explorations of philosophical concepts applied to modern life.
scoring_notes: Solid philosophy channel. Good at connecting ancient wisdom to modern problems. Production is clean but not flashy. Some videos cover well-trodden ground. Consistent quality.
sample_videos: [{"title": "Stoicism: Become Undefeatable", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EFkyxzJtiv4"}, {"title": "Nietzsche - How To Find Yourself", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=wHWbZmg2hzU"}]

name: Academy of Ideas
description: Classical philosophy and psychology animated. Nietzsche, Jung, Kierkegaard, and the great thinkers explained through their original texts and classical art.
scoring_notes: Faithful to primary sources. Uses classical art as visuals which works well. Some videos lean libertarian in interpretation. Good for encountering philosophical ideas directly.
sample_videos: [{"title": "Introduction to Carl Jung - Individuation", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=x01HmFo2cRM"}, {"title": "Nietzsche and Morality", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=w8-eQ4DLBYs"}]

name: Then & Now
description: Political philosophy, social theory, and intellectual history. Covers Foucault, Chomsky, Arendt, and modern political ideas with academic depth.
scoring_notes: Strong academic grounding in political philosophy. Covers thinkers rarely discussed on YouTube. Well-researched scripts. Production is good but not spectacular. Important niche.
sample_videos: [{"title": "What is Neoliberalism?", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pzVf9ce80Nc"}, {"title": "Hannah Arendt and the Banality of Evil", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MPlEHPB_BNQ"}]

name: Like Stories of Old
description: Tom van der Linden's film essays exploring philosophical themes through cinema. Connects movies to existential questions with gorgeous editing.
scoring_notes: Beautiful marriage of film analysis and philosophy. Editing and pacing are excellent. Some essays are emotionally powerful. Unique niche at the intersection of cinema and existentialism.
sample_videos: [{"title": "The Beauty of Arrival", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8YCGR_1J1IM"}, {"title": "The Philosophy of Interstellar", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=k6RWL1Ry8fY"}]

name: Sisyphus 55
description: Animated philosophy exploring existentialism, absurdism, and meaning. Camus, Sartre, and modern philosophy through hand-drawn animation.
scoring_notes: Good entry-level philosophy content. Animation style is charming. Covers the usual existentialist suspects. Less depth than academic channels but more accessible.
sample_videos: [{"title": "The Myth of Sisyphus - Albert Camus", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=R18e8tTBwno"}, {"title": "What Jean-Paul Sartre Meant by 'Hell Is Other People'", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=RhmHSAClG1c"}]

name: After Skool
description: Whiteboard animations of lectures by philosophers, psychologists, and thinkers. Alan Watts, Jordan Peterson, Terence McKenna visualized.
scoring_notes: Curation of existing lectures is the value-add. Whiteboard animation is well-executed. Content quality depends on the speaker featured. Some fringe speakers mixed with legitimate thinkers.
sample_videos: [{"title": "The Real Reason to Learn Another Language - Alan Watts", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GS0NOVQHmHI"}, {"title": "How to Stop Overthinking", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=s7NqPSqJdeQ"}]

name: Vsauce
description: Michael Stevens explores philosophical thought experiments, math paradoxes, and existential questions. 'Hey Vsauce, Michael here' is a YouTube cultural landmark.
scoring_notes: Pioneered the 'tangent-driven exploration' format. Each video is a journey through interconnected ideas. Research is obsessive. Some newer content (D!NG, Mind Field) is weaker. Classic videos are timeless.
sample_videos: [{"title": "What If Everyone Jumped At Once?", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jHbyQ_AQP8c"}, {"title": "Which Way Is Down?", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Xc4xYacTu-E"}]

name: The Futur
description: Chris Do's design business education. Pricing creative work, client negotiations, and design thinking. MBA-level business training for creatives.
scoring_notes: Unique intersection of design and business. Role-play negotiations are genuinely educational. Some content is promotional for courses. Strong community engagement.
sample_videos: [{"title": "How to Price Design Work", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=RKXZ7t_RiOE"}, {"title": "Stop Charging Hourly", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7gNtET5OJWI"}]

name: Nerdwriter1
description: Evan Puschak's video essays on art, film, music, and culture. Meticulous editing and thoughtful analysis of why creative works resonate.
scoring_notes: Editing style is distinctive and influential. Art analysis is accessible without being shallow. Music and film essays are strongest. Consistent quality over many years.
sample_videos: [{"title": "How Spielberg Directs", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8q4X2vDRfRk"}, {"title": "Art in the Age of Mechanical Reproduction", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cVuaXJ2LPXQ"}]

name: Every Frame a Painting
description: Tony Zhou's legendary film analysis channel. Though no longer active, set the standard for video essays. Editing, cinematography, and directing dissected.
scoring_notes: Defined the video essay genre. Every video is a masterclass in film analysis. No longer active but every video remains essential viewing. Spawned thousands of imitators.
sample_videos: [{"title": "Edgar Wright - How to Do Visual Comedy", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3FOzD4Sfgag"}, {"title": "The Marvel Symphonic Universe", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7vfqkvwW2fs"}]

name: Now You See It
description: Film and media analysis exploring tropes, genres, and storytelling techniques. Why certain film techniques work and how audiences respond to them.
scoring_notes: Solid film analysis. Good at identifying patterns across cinema. Production is clean. Some overlap with other film essay channels but maintains its own voice.
sample_videos: [{"title": "Why Do Characters Look at the Camera?", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EV91uCLbv7M"}, {"title": "The Problem with Movie Trailers", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5yqbG5TsT_Y"}]

name: Design Theory
description: Design principles, typography, and visual communication explained. Why certain designs work and the theory behind effective graphic design.
scoring_notes: Good fundamentals coverage. Typography content is well-done. Some videos feel like listicles. Useful for design students. Not groundbreaking but solid educational content.
sample_videos: [{"title": "The Rules of Typography", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QrNi9FmdlxY"}, {"title": "Why Helvetica is Everywhere", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=f0_R1qMRfek"}]

name: The Art Assignment
description: PBS-produced art history and criticism. Sarah Urist Green (curator) explains art movements, artists, and why art matters with museum-quality presentations.
scoring_notes: Professional art history on YouTube. Curator credentials add authority. PBS production values. 'The Case For' series is excellent. Good balance of analysis and accessibility.
sample_videos: [{"title": "The Case for Abstraction", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_fEfpz7rPR8"}, {"title": "Was Bob Ross a Good Artist?", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rDs3o1uLEdU"}]

name: Lessons from the Screenplay
description: Michael Tucker analyzes screenwriting techniques using specific films. Structure, dialogue, and character development dissected with film theory.
scoring_notes: Excellent screenwriting analysis. Each video teaches a specific storytelling technique. Well-produced with good film clips. Complements Every Frame a Painting for film study.
sample_videos: [{"title": "Arrival: Examining an Adaptation", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QJhLFfzIzR4"}, {"title": "Gone Girl: Don't Underestimate the Screenwriter", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CF2o5RDkq9A"}]

name: CrashCourse
description: Hank and John Green's educational empire. Complete courses in history, biology, chemistry, CS, and more. Structured curriculum with consistent quality.
scoring_notes: Incredible breadth of topics covered. Each series is a mini-course. Some series are stronger than others. Not the deepest but reliably accurate. Massive educational impact.
sample_videos: [{"title": "The Agricultural Revolution: Crash Course World History #1", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Yocja_N5s1I"}, {"title": "Computer Science Crash Course Preview", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=tpIctyqH29Q"}]

name: TED-Ed
description: TED's education channel. Animated lessons from expert educators on everything from history to science to philosophy. Each video is a self-contained lesson.
scoring_notes: Consistently high production with varied animation styles. Expert educators ensure accuracy. Topics range widely. Individual videos are excellent but don't build a curriculum.
sample_videos: [{"title": "How does the stock market work?", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=p7HKvqRI_Bo"}, {"title": "The philosophy of Stoicism", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=R9OCA6UFE-0"}]

name: Primer
description: Simulations of evolution, economics, and game theory using blob creatures. Custom-coded simulations that demonstrate emergent behavior and complex systems.
scoring_notes: Highly original approach to teaching complex systems. Custom simulation software is impressive. Low upload frequency but each video is unique. Game theory content is particularly strong.
sample_videos: [{"title": "Simulating Natural Selection", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0ZGbIKd0XrM"}, {"title": "Simulating the Evolution of Aggression", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=YNMkADpvO4w"}]

name: Brilliant
description: Math and science learning platform's YouTube channel. Puzzles, problem-solving approaches, and mathematical thinking in short format.
scoring_notes: YouTube channel is mainly promotional for the platform. Some genuinely interesting puzzles. Content quality is decent but feels like marketing. The actual platform is better than the channel.
sample_videos: [{"title": "The Most Beautiful Equation in Math", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IUTGFQpKaPU"}, {"title": "Can you solve this riddle?", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IkfmI6cqoP4"}]

name: Science Etonnante
description: David Louapre, docteur en physique, vulgarise la science en francais. Mecanique quantique, relativite, maths et biologie expliques avec rigueur et clarte.
scoring_notes: Meilleure chaine science francophone. Rigueur academique sans sacrifier l'accessibilite. PhD en physique des particules. Chaque video est verifiable. Reference absolue en FR.
sample_videos: [{"title": "La Mecanique Quantique en 7 Idees", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Rj3jTw2DxXQ"}, {"title": "Le Paradoxe des Jumeaux", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XKjOm5VTn_s"}]

name: Heu?reka
description: Economie et finance expliquees en francais. Gilles, ancien trader, decortique la finance, les bulles speculatives et les mecanismes economiques avec humour.
scoring_notes: Expertise financiere reelle (ancien trader). Humour distinctif. Explications des mecanismes financiers rares en francais. Quelques videos longues mais le contenu justifie la duree.
sample_videos: [{"title": "La Bourse pour les Nuls", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sSnoIk2rOZ4"}, {"title": "Bitcoin - Comprendre et Investir?", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kv3zuMXMfHI"}]

name: Polymathematic
description: Math applied to real-world problems: game show strategy, pizza cutting, and probability puzzles. Makes mathematical reasoning fun and practical.
scoring_notes: Entertaining math applications. Good at connecting abstract math to daily life. Production is clean. Topics are well-chosen for broad appeal. Growing channel with potential.
sample_videos: [{"title": "The Math Behind Game Shows", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=TVq2ivVpZgQ"}, {"title": "Why Pizza Is a Lie", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ERYnRHJjN-8"}]

name: Zach Star
description: Applied math and engineering education. How math is used in real engineering, physics simulations, and career advice for STEM students.
scoring_notes: Good at showing real applications of abstract math. Engineering career content is practical. Production is solid. Some videos overlap with other math channels but applied focus differentiates.
sample_videos: [{"title": "The Math Major", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=PrU-fCNiJco"}, {"title": "How Imaginary Numbers Were Invented", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cUzklzVXJwo"}]

name: Our Changing Climate
description: Environmental video essays examining climate change, sustainability, and systemic solutions. Well-researched with beautiful visuals and measured tone.
scoring_notes: Thoughtful environmental content that avoids doom-scrolling. Well-researched with good sources. Visual storytelling is strong. Addresses systemic issues not just individual action.
sample_videos: [{"title": "Is Your Car Making You Miserable?", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6-0bVs9oGkM"}, {"title": "How We Ended Up Living in a Parking Lot", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Kd-0FJwJSmI"}]

name: Climate Town
description: Rollie Williams uses comedy and investigative journalism to explain climate issues. Fossil fuel lobbying, greenwashing, and policy failures with sharp humor.
scoring_notes: Comedy makes heavy topics accessible. Well-researched investigative content. Some videos are more entertainment than education. Greenwashing exposures are particularly strong.
sample_videos: [{"title": "Who Killed the Electric Car (Again)?", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2_wO9e4gJNo"}, {"title": "The Greatest Greenwashing Scam of All Time", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3iJnMJvM8A4"}]

name: Just Have a Think
description: Dave Borlace covers energy transition technology weekly. Batteries, hydrogen, nuclear, solar innovations explained with engineering perspective.
scoring_notes: Consistent weekly coverage of energy tech. Engineering background adds credibility. Production is basic but content is substantive. Good at covering emerging technologies early.
sample_videos: [{"title": "Solid State Batteries - A Complete Guide", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0_fSNBk28FI"}, {"title": "Nuclear Fusion - Is It Finally Here?", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=p_T4dYsBi5o"}]

name: Not Just Bikes
description: Jason Slaughter compares North American and Dutch urban design. How car-centric planning fails cities and what walkable, bikeable alternatives look like.
scoring_notes: Single-handedly made urban planning interesting to millions. Strong Opinions Loosely Held series with StrongTowns is excellent. Moved from Amsterdam gives credible comparison perspective.
sample_videos: [{"title": "Why City Design is Important (and Why I Hate Houston)", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=uxykI30fS54"}, {"title": "The Best Country in the World for Drivers", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=d8RRE2rDw4k"}]

name: Kurzgesagt - Environment
description: Climate scientist Adam Levy explains climate research findings. PhD in atmospheric physics bringing actual peer-reviewed science to YouTube.
scoring_notes: Real climate scientist credentials. Research-based content with proper citations. Production is modest but science is solid. Important voice in climate communication.
sample_videos: [{"title": "What 1.5 Degrees Actually Means", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fHmKlB_dNwU"}, {"title": "Is Nuclear Power Green?", "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=k13jZ9qHJ5U"}]

name: Primitive Technology
description: John Plant builds shelters, tools, and kilns from scratch in the Australian bush. Completely silent videos with captions. No tools, no shortcuts, pure making.
scoring_notes: Defined an entire genre of YouTube content. Zero narration, zero filler. Each build demonstrates real archaeological/engineering knowledge. Timeless content that will be watched for generations.

name: Adam Savage's Tested
